name: cds_option_payer_atm_3m
description: Bloomberg CDSO IBM single-name 3M payer (call) option struck at the ATM forward CDS spread.

provenance.source_detail: Bloomberg CDSO IBM USD senior 5Y CDS option, S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid, IBM USD Senior Curve, valuation date 2026-05-02, ATM payer struck at forward 58.3954bp, Spread Vol 81.02%
provenance.regen_command: manual Bloomberg CDSO/CDSW/CDSD screen transcription; see provenance.screenshots

screenshots.description: Bloomberg CDSO Credit Default Swaption screen for IBM 3M ATM payer with strike 58.3954bp, spread vol 81.02%, market value 31177.82, IR DV01 -5.77, Spread DV01 2563.44, Vega(1%) 381.02, Theta -309.08
Bloomberg CDSW underlying single-name CDS terms (IBM USD senior, 2014 SNAC XR14, recovery 0.40, S531 swap curve, IBM USD Senior credit curve)
S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid benchmark rates, identical to the curve used for the CDSW pricing screen

meta.swap_curve: S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid

source_reference.date_convention_note: Operational CDS option dates are derived by the model: cash settlement is valuation/trade date adjusted to the next business day plus the underlying CDS convention settlement lag; exercise settlement is two business days before option expiry; underlying CDS accrual start is the calendar day after the prior CDS roll date.
source_reference.discount_curve_note: S531 USD ISDA Swap Curve - Mid; discount factors transcribed from the Bloomberg CDSW premium cashflow schedule for the IBM 5Y CDS golden (cds_5y_par_spread.json) and reused for option discounting.
source_reference.hazard_curve_note: IBM USD Senior credit curve par spreads transcribed from the Bloomberg CDSO/CDSD MSG1 Mid screen; hazard knots are bootstrapped on-demand from those par spreads using finstack's CDS pricer + Brent root finding (sequential per-pillar, IMM-rolled maturities, knot times in act/365f years). The bootstrap is deterministic and reproduced by the diagnostic unit test `instruments::credit_derivatives::cds_option::pricer::tests::diag_cds_option_payer_atm_3m` (run with --include-ignored). Sanity: spot 5Y par on the bootstrapped curve = 57.000000bp (matches the input par_spread). The synthetic forward CDS par spread matches Bloomberg ATM Forward 58.3954bp to within 0.30%.
source_reference.vol_surface_note: Bloomberg displays a single Spread Vol of 81.02% for this option; the surface is intentionally a flat 81.02% surface so that the model picks up the screen-quoted volatility regardless of strike or expiry interpolation. The pricing override implied_volatility is also set to 0.8102 for determinism.
transcription_notes: Bloomberg CDSO Spread Vol field is shown in percent and equals 81.02; pricing_overrides.implied_volatility is set to the decimal equivalent 0.8102 to pin the lognormal vol used by the Black-on-spreads pricer.

The synthetic underlying CDS uses Bloomberg's underlying effective date (2026-06-21) as the premium accrual start and uses option expiry (2026-06-26) as the protection start, matching the CDSO forward-start convention shown on the screen.

Delta (% = N(d1)) and Gamma (+/-5bp) are Bloomberg display conventions whose units differ from the registered finstack delta/gamma metrics; they are intentionally excluded from the metrics list and live in bloomberg_outputs for human reference only.

Hazard knots were rebuilt by sequential par-spread bootstrap (Brent root finding, IMM-rolled maturities, knot times in act/365f years). With these self-consistent knots, the CDSO underlying-effective-date convention, and BloombergCdswClean valuation convention on the synthetic underlying CDS, finstack's synthetic forward CDS par spread is 58.392bp vs Bloomberg ATM Forward 58.3954bp (0.006% drift).

DV01 uses a CDS-option-specific calculator (CdsOptionDv01Calculator) that re-bootstraps the hazard curve from the par-spread term structure under the bumped discount curve, matching the corresponding CDS DV01 calculator and the Bloomberg CDSO IR DV01 convention. Without this, IR DV01 was ~3.5x too large because the rate bump was implicitly perturbing the implied par spreads as well.

Expected outputs use Bloomberg screen values directly. Tolerances reflect the residual model-vs-screen convention drift after Phase A/B/C alignment work; documented finstack-vs-Bloomberg drift lives in finstack_reconciliation.

finstack_reconciliation.expected_outputs_policy: Bloomberg screen values for displayed metrics. Tolerances reflect the residual finstack-vs-Bloomberg convention drift after the CDSO Phase A/B/C alignment work (BloombergCdswClean synthetic underlying + inclusive-end Black-T rule). Deltas vs. Bloomberg are documented below for visibility into model-vs-market drift; the absolute tolerances are sized to allow the residual without re-introducing source_validation.
model_fixes_applied: Replaced transcribed hazard knots (~1.017% IBM survival-probability-derived) with self-consistent knots bootstrapped from the par-spread term structure (Brent root finding, IMM-rolled maturities). Drops forward-spread drift from 3.7% to 0.30% and NPV drift from 23.0% to 7.8%.

Added CdsOptionDv01Calculator that re-bootstraps the hazard curve from par_spreads under the bumped discount curve before repricing (Bloomberg CDSO convention), replacing the generic UnifiedDv01Calculator(parallel_combined). Drops IR DV01 magnitude from -20.54 (256% drift) to -9.35 (62% drift).
Threaded Bloomberg's underlying effective date into the synthetic CDSO underlying and switched the CDSO Black annuity to the full premium-leg PV01 including expected accrued premium on default.

Switched the synthetic underlying CDS valuation_convention from IsdaDirty to BloombergCdswClean. CDSW uses adjusted-to-adjusted accrual periods plus the +1-day rule on the final ACT/360 period; this lifts the option's effective annuity from 4.5061 to 4.5084 (closer to Bloomberg-implied 4.51552).

Extended CDSOption.black_time_to_expiry to count +1 day inclusive of the end date, mirroring the Bloomberg CDSW final-period convention. With ACT/360 day count, this makes the option's Black T = 49/360 = 0.13611 (vs the old 48/360 = 0.13333 and Bloomberg-implied ~0.13481).
finstack_reconciliation.black_time_to_expiry_note: ACT/360 with inclusive-end +1d rule: 49 days / 360 = 0.13611. Bloomberg implied T ≈ 0.13481 from back-solving NPV with annuity = 4.51552, F = K = 58.3954bp, σ = 0.8102. The +0.4% T overshoot is the dominant driver of the +0.06% NPV overshoot.
finstack_reconciliation.npv_note: Residual +0.06% (~$19.55) drift after Phase A/B/C alignment work (BloombergCdswClean synthetic underlying + inclusive-end +1d Black T). Down from -$58.33 in the prior IsdaDirty + 48d/360 setup. Closing further requires either a black_time_to_expiry_override or matching Bloomberg's exact hazard-bootstrap micro-conventions; both intentionally deferred.
finstack_reconciliation.vega_note: Vega(1%) overshoots by ~0.67%; same T-convention root cause as NPV.
finstack_reconciliation.dv01_note: Bloomberg-style IR DV01: bump rates by +/-1bp parallel, re-bootstrap hazard from the unchanged par-spread term structure, reprice option (CdsOptionDv01Calculator). The 29% relative drift on the $-5.77 base translates to ~$1.7 absolute; further closure requires reproducing Bloomberg's exact rate-bump and hazard-bootstrap conventions.
finstack_reconciliation.cs01_note: Finstack CS01 is the parallel par-spread bump + hazard re-bootstrap CS01 (GenericParallelCs01) under the BloombergCdswClean synthetic underlying. The remaining ~2.5% drift comes from Bloomberg's CDSO Spread DV01 implying ∂F/∂S ≈ 1.017 (likely a 5Y key-rate bump) versus finstack's parallel ~1.0004; user accepted this convention residual (Q3 → C3 in the alignment plan).
finstack_reconciliation.theta_note: Finstack theta is the 1-calendar-day forward finite-difference theta (PV(as_of+1d) - PV(as_of)). The ~2.0% drift vs. Bloomberg sits between the NPV and CS01 residuals.

risk_metric_notes.npv: Bloomberg Market Value is the option premium in USD: premium_pct/100 x notional = 0.311778% x 10,000,000 = 31,177.80 (small rounding vs. screen). Finstack NPV is the discounted Black-on-spreads payer-option PV with BloombergCdswClean synthetic underlying CDS and inclusive-end Black T (49d/360).
risk_metric_notes.vega: Bloomberg Vega(1%) is the dollar PV change for a +1 absolute percentage point move in lognormal spread vol. Matches finstack vega definition (per 1 vol point).
risk_metric_notes.dv01: Bloomberg IR DV01 is the +1bp parallel discount-curve bump impact on option PV with par CDS spreads held constant via hazard re-bootstrap. Matches finstack CdsOptionDv01Calculator (added in this fix), mirroring CdsDv01Calculator for the underlying.
risk_metric_notes.cs01: Bloomberg Spread DV01 is the +1bp par-spread bump on the credit curve with hazard re-bootstrap. Matches finstack parallel par-spread CS01 (GenericParallelCs01) which is the registered MetricId::Cs01 for CDSOption. Bloomberg's effective ∂F/∂S ≈ 1.017 (likely a 5Y key-rate bump) vs finstack's parallel ≈ 1.0004 — the residual was accepted by the alignment plan (Q3 → C3).
risk_metric_notes.theta: Bloomberg Theta is the 1-calendar-day forward time decay in dollars. Matches finstack theta_finite_diff (PV(as_of+1d) - PV(as_of)).

npv.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Market Value in USD to cents; sized to absorb the residual ~$20 (~0.06%) drift after the CDSO Phase A/B/C alignment work (BloombergCdswClean synthetic underlying + inclusive-end +1d Black T rule). Closing this further requires either reproducing Bloomberg's exact CDSO Black T convention (no public spec) or adding a black_time_to_expiry_override; both intentionally deferred per the alignment plan.

vega.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Vega(1%) to cents; widened to absorb the residual ~$2 (~0.57%) drift; same root cause as NPV (T-convention overshoot under inclusive-end rule).

dv01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays IR DV01 to cents; the small absolute base ($-5.77) means even a 30% relative drift translates to <$2. Closing further requires Bloomberg's exact rate-bump and hazard-bootstrap conventions.

cs01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Spread DV01 to cents; the residual ~$68 (~2.6%) drift comes from Bloomberg's CDSO Spread DV01 implying ∂F/∂S ≈ 1.017 (likely 5Y key-rate bump) vs finstack's parallel ~1.0004. Convention residual accepted (Q3 → C3).

theta.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg displays Theta per day to cents; the residual ~$6 (~2.0%) drift sits between the NPV and CS01 residuals; same convention root causes.
